what_it_does: Renders a static design-system preview card showing sidebar navigation item states: default, hover, and active, with explanatory legend text.
observations: Includes a dsCard metadata comment with group Brand, name Sidebar nav states, subtitle Default · hover · dashed-gold active, and viewport 700x280.
Loads shared styling from _card.css and defines file-local CSS for the card layout, nav, items, glyphs, and legend.
Uses a two-column grid with a 220px sidebar preview and a text legend.
Shows four nav items, with Projects and Brand Kit in default state, Dashboard Updates in hover state, and Gallery in active state.
Active state uses a white surface, 1.5px dashed border using --border-dashed, and adjusted padding.
Hover state uses --bg-surface and --shadow-xs without changing text color.
Uses emoji glyphs as nav icons.
standouts: Dashed gold active outline is explicitly described as the brand's selection treatment.
The file documents states visually and textually in the same preview card.
State examples are represented with static classes rather than interactive behavior.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties for colors, radius, shadow, and font tokens.
Keeps component-specific styling inline in the preview HTML.
Uses semantic state class names: default, hover, and active.
Uses span elements for nav items, indicating this is a visual specimen rather than a functional navigation component.
concerns: Nav items are cursor-styled but not keyboard-focusable or linked in this static markup.
Emoji glyph rendering may vary by platform.
The hover state is hard-coded as a class rather than demonstrated through an actual :hover rule.
notes: This file is a compact visual ledger card for sidebar navigation states and their design-token styling.
questions: Should the active dashed border always use --border-dashed, or is that token specifically gold in the shared stylesheet?
Are emoji glyphs intended as final iconography or placeholders for the preview?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated sidebar nav states purpose matches the rendered preview and legend.
intention_signals: It seems intended to communicate how sidebar navigation items should look across default, hover, and active states in a design-system handoff.
novelty: The active state combines a white fill with a dashed gold outline as a distinctive selection treatment.
contribution: Provides a concrete sidebar navigation state specimen with visual examples, token usage, and legend copy for default, hover, and active item treatments.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML design-system preview card for ConceptV sidebar navigation states, showing default, hover, and active item styling with a legend.
intention_for_embedding: Created to document and visually demonstrate sidebar nav state treatments, especially the dashed-gold active selection style.
